assumption_note: Gingerbread-man-shaped cookie, not a bread loaf -- checked before the bare "BREAD" pattern below specifically because "GINGERBREAD" contains "bread" as a substring (real bug caught this way: it was matching the 20 oz loaf proxy and implying $0.36/lb, obviously wrong for a cookie). Used a small cookie proxy at 1.5 oz.
Standard foodservice bagel proxy at 2.5 oz each.

Whole quick-bread loaf (banana nut, citrus, ginger, marble, pumpkin -- all Pack size 1, i.e. Quantity is already an individual-loaf count) at a standard ~20 oz loaf-pan proxy. Word-bounded specifically so it doesn't match "GINGERBREAD" as a substring (see the dedicated entry above).
Standard bakery cookie proxy at 2.0 oz each -- commercial chocolate chip cookies typically run 2-3 oz.

Catch-all for remaining pastry-style items (cinnamon twist, morning bun, cinnamon roll, bear claw, fruit/cheese pastries) not matched by a more specific pattern above -- a rougher proxy than the named categories, lowest confidence in this file.
